task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly drops the minimum and maximum values, calculates the mean of the remaining numbers, handles empty lists, and has some minor room for improvement in terms of conciseness.
trace: I first checked if the input list is empty and returned None for that edge case. Then, I sorted the input list and determined the indices of the minimum and maximum numbers, considering an edge case where the minimum or maximum number occurs only once in the list. Lastly, I calculated the sum of the numbers at the selected indices and divided it by the number of elements to compute the trimmed mean. The code is concise and readable while handling the mentioned edge case.
